user_message: Just got this from David Rivera at Fourth Coffee -- I need help figuring out security posture. Board meeting is next week.

signals.detail: Signal detected for Fourth Coffee: the migration can proceed in phases to reduce risk

required_artifacts: innovate) with success criteria per phase

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF20-J trap (Treats modernization as one-time, ignores iterative reality); produces complete Phased modernization plan (assess, migrate, optimize, innovate) with success criteria per phase, fun
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF20-J failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF20-J failure mode (Treats modernization as one-time, ignores iterative reality)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct security posture decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF20-J -- Treats modernization as one-time, ignores iterative reality
